Vulnerabilities with Remediation

Banque&Data_Mars
App Name: Banque&Data_MarsScan Config: ProductionScan Date: 2026-03-13 13:14:42Report Generated: 2026-03-13 13:56:58

37m 20s

Duration of Scan

Scan Completed - 2026-03-13 13:52:02

137

Crawled Links

Logged Out

192

Vulnerabilities Discovered

29832 - Attacks Performed

Vulnerabilities by Severity

Attack Type

JavaScriptLeaks (40)

References

OWASP2025-A02

Description

The default, non-strict mode allows some relatively "unsafe" actions to be taken, and it allows some code mistakes that make it difficult for JavaScript engines to perform optimizations.

Recommendation

To prevent these mistakes from happening, add 'use strict'; at the beginning of your JavaScript files. This enables a stricter mode of parsing JavaScript that prevents accidental globals. Strict mode makes several changes to normal JavaScript semantics. First, It prevents, or throws errors, when relatively "unsafe" actions are taken. Second, strict mode eliminates some JavaScript silent errors by changing them to throw errors.

Vector String

AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N

Host: admin.mars.doca-easi.fr
Referer: https://admin.mars.doca-easi.fr/
X-RTC-REQUESTID: {AEB92096-A9F6-44A7-B675-2376DEBC0112}

X-Content-Type-Options (20)

References

OWASP2023API-0xa8 OWASP2025-A06 CWE-693

Description

The only defined value, "nosniff", prevents Internet Explorer and Google Chrome from MIME-sniffing a response away from the declared content-type. This also applies to Google Chrome, when downloading extensions. This reduces exposure to drive-by download attacks and sites serving user uploaded content that, by clever naming, could be treated by MSIE as executable or dynamic HTML files.

Recommendation

The X-Content-Type-Options HTTP response header can be used to indicate whether or not a browser should be allowed to sniff a response away from the declared content-type. Sites can use this to avoid MIME-sniffing a response away from the declared content-type.

Vector String

AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N/E:P/RL:X/RC:C

Attack Information

  • Response
GET / HTTP/1.1
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Accept-Language: en-US
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/110.0.5481.180 Safari/537.36
X-RTC-AUTH: R7_IAS
X-RTC-SCANID: ce6bae66-07c4-4879-839f-e55e7427b0ff
Host: admin.mars.doca-easi.fr
X-RTC-REQUESTID: {B0C5D719-6585-4C45-A4F5-9F0950204D31}

HTTPHeaders (105)

References

CWE-79 OWASP2023API-0xa8 OWASP2025-A05

Description

There have been vulnerabilities related to the charset attribute and content-type options found. Vulnerabilities found can include:
The use of the Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), when used inappropriately, can cause issues with parsing and processing data, leading to unexpected behavior and potential security weaknesses.
The page's character set does not match the recommended UTF-8 character-set. Inconsistent character encoding can enable various types of attacks like Cross-Site Scripting and potential denial of services.
Checks for a missing charset attribute. This can lead to the browser making assumptions about the character encoding, which can lead to parsing errors and security vulnerabilities.

Recommendation

Always declare the character encoding of all text documents (html, text, javascript, json, stylesheet, etc).
Always use an in-document declaration for HTML responses.
Remove the BOM unless it is explicitly required.
Align the character set with UTF-8 encoding.

Vector String

AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N

Attack Information

Host: mars.doca-easi.fr

X-RTC-REQUESTID: {7BDDB5FA-CBB8-46AE-9BA6-2A414A329415}

CSPHeaders (21)

References

OWASP2025-A02 OWASP2017-A5 OWASP2021-A05 CWE-16

Description

The Content Security Policy hasn't been declared properly either through the meta-tag or the HTTP header, so the browser's trust of the content received from the server can be exploited. Malicious scripts are executed by the victim's browser because the browser trusts the source of the content, even when it's not coming from where it seems to be coming from.

Recommendation

The Content-Security-Policy HTTP response header helps you reduce XSS risks on modern browsers by declaring what dynamic resources are allowed to load via a HTTP Header. CSP makes it possible for server administrators to reduce or eliminate the vectors by which XSS can occur by specifying the domains that the browser should consider to be valid sources of executable scripts. A CSP compatible browser will then only execute scripts loaded in source files received from those allowlisted domains, ignoring all other script (including inline scripts and event-handling HTML attributes).

CVSS Score

3.8

Vector String

AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N/E:P/RL:X/RC:R

Attack Information

X-RTC-REQUESTID: {9D4A55AF-C804-48DD-9F95-53D5DE4A4BD2}

AnonymousAccessType (2)

References

OWASP2025-A01 CWE-284 OWASP2021-A01 OWASP2023API-0xa5

Description

The presence of this vulnerability allows any user to access or post content without providing a user name/password or security token challenge.

Recommendation

Disable Anonymous Authentication in the server configuration.

Vector String

AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:N

Injection Sinks (3)

References

CWE-116 OVAL-6312 OWASP2021-A03 OWASP2017-A1 CAPEC-152 OWASP2025-A05

Description

A known injection sink was found. Javascript methods such as eval(), document.write(), and innerHTML or outerHTML can interpret HTML, JSON and Javascript input as code. As a result these are vectors for injection attacks such as cross-site scripting (XSS) when their input is not trusted and properly sanitized.

Recommendation

Prefer secure parsing methods: Do not assign input directly to the DOM or evaluate it as code. For example, if the input should be JSON, prefer secure methods such as JSON.parse over methods like eval() or .innerHTML

Use a robust HTML sanitization library to strip out any potentially malicious scripts or attributes

Inputs used to construct the DOM should come from only trusted and controlled sources. Values that originate from client-side inputs or external APIs should be treated as potential attack vectors

CVSS Score

3.7

Vector String

AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:L/I:L/A:N
